name: hwm_anchored_stop_breakeven_floor_holds_post_lock
description: AC-3 (H-1): once breakeven latches, the resolved stop_trigger_level floors at 0.0 even when the HWM-anchored base goes negative (deep vol spike widens the distance past the HWM). The 0.0 breakeven floor is the ONLY ratchet that survives the H-1 fix — 'lock gains hard'. The stop may still move down between post-lock ticks, but never below 0.0.
derivation: currently_breakeven_locked=True (lock already latched on a prior tick). symphony_vol=1.0 -> threshold=0.8. Tick 1: current_return 2.0 >= 0.8 -> hwm_hold_ticks 5->6; locked stays True; base = HWM(3.0) - distance(0.5) = 2.5; locked -> stop = max(2.5, 0.0) = 2.5. Tick 2: current_return 2.0 -> ticks 6->7; base = HWM(3.0) - distance(4.0) = -1.0 (distance widened past the HWM); locked -> stop = max(-1.0, 0.0) = 0.0 (floored). Expected: stop drops 2.5 -> 0.0 but is floored at 0.0, never negative. A pre-fix impl would clamp to the prior level 2.5; a broken impl that dropped the max(base,0.0) floor would emit -1.0.
invariant: post-lock stop_trigger_level >= 0.0 always; it may move down but the 0.0 breakeven floor is the hard guarantee
